prompt: Tell me a bio of Kang Ji-hwan
evaluation_criteria: Factual accuracy of nationality (South Korean), profession (actor), notable dramas and films, acting debut (The Moon is the Sun's Dream, 1992), career timeline. Verify specific film/TV appearances rather than accepting vague claims about charity work or career achievements not mentioned in Wikipedia.
output:
## Biography of Kang Ji-hwan

**Kang Ji-hwan** (Korean: 강지환; born March 20, 1977, in Seoul, South Korea), birth name **Jo Tae-gyu** (조태규), is a South Korean **former actor** whose career spanned from 2002 to 2019. He is known for a prolific career in both television dramas and film, as well as musical theatre.

---

### Early Life & Career Beginnings (2002–2004)

Kang made his acting debut in **musical theatre** in 2002, appearing in Korean stagings of *The Rocky Horror Show* and *Grease*. Using the stage name "Kang Ji-hwan," he transitioned to television with small roles in the sitcom *Nonstop 4* (2003) and dramas *Summer Scent* (2003) and *Save the Last Dance for Me* (2004). In *Grease* (2004), he played the lead role of Danny Zuko.

---

### Breakthrough & Rising Fame (2005–2008)

Kang shot to fame in **2005** with his first onscreen leading role in the MBC drama **"Be Strong, Geum-soon!"**, playing a fastidious doctor who falls for a widowed hairdresser (Han Hye-jin). The drama was a hit and boosted his pan-Asian popularity, especially in Japan, China, and Taiwan. He won **MBC Drama Awards** for Excellence Award (Actor) and Best New Actor that year.

He made his **big-screen debut** in the independent film *Host and Guest* (2006), which traveled the international film festival circuit.

After a lull with low-rated dramas *Fireworks* (2006) and *90 Days, Time to Love* (2006), Kang rebounded with **"Capital Scandal"** (2007), a well-reviewed period drama set in Japanese-occupied Korea, co-starring Han Ji-min.

**2008** was a landmark year:
- He starred as the titular folk hero in **"Hong Gil-dong"**, written by the famous Hong Sisters, which became a cult hit.
- He co-starred with **So Ji-sub** in the critically acclaimed film **"Rough Cut"** (2008), directed by Jang Hoon and written by Kim Ki-duk. Both actors won multiple **Best New Actor** awards, including from the Blue Dragon Film Awards, Baeksang Arts Awards, and the Korean Association of Film Critics.

---

### Commercial Peak (2009–2012)

Kang starred opposite Kim Ha-neul in the action-romantic comedy **"My Girlfriend Is an Agent"** (2009), which became the **4th highest-grossing Korean film of 2009** with over 4 million tickets sold.

He headlined the romantic comedy drama **"Coffee House"** (2010) and returned to musical theatre with *Cafe In* (2010), becoming the **first Korean actor to perform at the Tokyo Globe Theatre** in Japan.

In 2011, he co-starred with **Yoon Eun-hye** in **"Lie to Me"**, playing a hotelier entangled in a fake marriage scheme. He followed up with the comedy film **"Runway Cop"** (2012), for which he famously gained 12 kg and then lost 15 kg in two weeks for his role.

---

### Later Career (2013–2018)

- **"Incarnation of Money"** (2013) — played a prosecutor with ties to a loan shark.
- **"Big Man"** (2014) — an orphan tricked into believing he's a wealthy heir, only to discover they want his heart for transplant.
- **"Monster"** (2016) — a revenge melodrama reuniting with co-star Sung Yu-ri.
- **"Children of a Lesser God"** (2018) — his first cable drama, playing a genius detective.
- **"Feel Good to Die"** (2018) — a workplace comedy.

---

### Awards & Recognition

Kang was a decorated actor, winning numerous awards including:
- **45th Baeksang Arts Awards** — Best New Actor (Film) for *Rough Cut* (2009)
- **29th Blue Dragon Film Awards** — Best New Actor for *Rough Cut* (2008)
- **44th Baeksang Arts Awards** — Most Popular Actor (TV) for *Hong Gil-dong* (2008)
- **KBS Drama Awards** — Excellence Award for *Capital Scandal* (2007) and multiple nominations
- **46th Grand Bell Awards** — Best New Actor for *My Girlfriend Is an Agent* (2009)
- **MBC Drama Awards** — Excellence Award & Best New Actor for *Be Strong, Geum-soon!* (2005)
- **9th Asia Model Awards** — Asia Star Award (2014)

---

### Contract Disputes

Kang's career was marred by protracted contract disputes:
- In **2010**, his former agency Jambo Entertainment sued him for breach of contract after he moved to S-Plus Entertainment. The Corea Entertainment Management Association (CEMA) suspended his activities for 8 months.
- In **2012**, S-Plus attempted to extend his contract by 8 months citing lost time. Kang refused, and S-Plus sued. In **2013**, the Seoul Central District Court **dismissed the lawsuit** and ordered S-Plus to pay Kang ₩5 million in damages for defamation.

---

### Sexual Assault Scandal & Retirement (2019)

On **July 9, 2019**, Kang was arrested for allegedly sexually molesting and assaulting **two female employees** of his agency at his home. He initially stated he remembered drinking with them but not what happened afterwards. On **July 15, 2019**, he **admitted to all charges** and apologized to the victims. His agency, Huayi Brothers, **terminated his contract** the following day, and Kang **retired from the entertainment industry**.

On **July 26, 2019**, he was formally indicted on charges of quasi-rape and quasi-indecent acts by

In *Grease* (2004), he played the lead role of Danny Zuko.

---

### Breakthrough & Rising Fame (2005–2008)

Kang shot to fame in **2005** with his first onscreen leading role in the MBC drama **"Be Strong, Geum-soon!"**, playing a fastidious doctor who falls for a widowed hairdresser (Han Hye-jin). The drama was a hit and boosted his pan-Asian popularity, especially in Japan, China, and Taiwan. He won **MBC Drama Awards** for Excellence Award (Actor) and Best New Actor that year.

He made his **big-screen debut** in the independent film *Host and Guest* (2006), which traveled the international film festival circuit.

After a lull with low-rated dramas *Fireworks* (2006) and *90 Days, Time to Love* (2006), Kang rebounded with **"Capital Scandal"** (2007), a well-reviewed period drama set in Japanese-occupied Korea, co-starring Han Ji-min.

**2008** was a landmark year:
- He starred as the titular folk hero in **"Hong Gil-dong"**, written by the famous Hong Sisters, which became a cult hit.
- He co-starred with **So Ji-sub** in the critically acclaimed film **"Rough Cut"** (2008), directed by Jang Hoon and written by Kim Ki-duk. Both actors won multiple **Best New Actor** awards, including from the Blue Dragon Film Awards, Baeksang Arts Awards, and the Korean Association of Film Critics.

---

### Commercial Peak (2009–2012)

Kang starred opposite Kim Ha-neul in the action-romantic comedy **"My Girlfriend Is an Agent"** (2009), which became the **4th highest-grossing Korean film of 2009** with over 4 million tickets sold.

He headlined the romantic comedy drama **"Coffee House"** (2010) and returned to musical theatre with *Cafe In* (2010), becoming the **first Korean actor to perform at the Tokyo Globe Theatre** in Japan.

In 2011, he co-starred with **Yoon Eun-hye** in **"Lie to Me"**, playing a hotelier entangled in a fake marriage scheme. He followed up with the comedy film **"Runway Cop"** (2012), for which he famously gained 12 kg and then lost 15 kg in two weeks for his role.

---

### Later Career (2013–2018)

- **"Incarnation of Money"** (2013) — played a prosecutor with ties to a loan shark.
- **"Big Man"** (2014) — an orphan tricked into believing he's a wealthy heir, only to discover they want his heart for transplant.
- **"Monster"** (2016) — a revenge melodrama reuniting with co-star Sung Yu-ri.
- **"Children of a Lesser God"** (2018) — his first cable drama, playing a genius detective.
- **"Feel Good to Die"** (2018) — a workplace comedy.

---

### Awards & Recognition

Kang was a decorated actor, winning numerous awards including:
- **45th Baeksang Arts Awards** — Best New Actor (Film) for *Rough Cut* (2009)
- **29th Blue Dragon Film Awards** — Best New Actor for *Rough Cut* (2008)
- **44th Baeksang Arts Awards** — Most Popular Actor (TV) for *Hong Gil-dong* (2008)
- **KBS Drama Awards** — Excellence Award for *Capital Scandal* (2007) and multiple nominations
- **46th Grand Bell Awards** — Best New Actor for *My Girlfriend Is an Agent* (2009)
- **MBC Drama Awards** — Excellence Award & Best New Actor for *Be Strong, Geum-soon!* (2005)
- **9th Asia Model Awards** — Asia Star Award (2014)

---

### Contract Disputes

Kang's career was marred by protracted contract disputes:
- In **2010**, his former agency Jambo Entertainment sued him for breach of contract after he moved to S-Plus Entertainment. The Corea Entertainment Management Association (CEMA) suspended his activities for 8 months.
- In **2012**, S-Plus attempted to extend his contract by 8 months citing lost time. Kang refused, and S-Plus sued. In **2013**, the Seoul Central District Court **dismissed the lawsuit** and ordered S-Plus to pay Kang ₩5 million in damages for defamation.

---

### Sexual Assault Scandal & Retirement (2019)

On **July 9, 2019**, Kang was arrested for allegedly sexually molesting and assaulting **two female employees** of his agency at his home. He initially stated he remembered drinking with them but not what happened afterwards. On **July 15, 2019**, he **admitted to all charges** and apologized to the victims. His agency, Huayi Brothers, **terminated his contract** the following day, and Kang **retired from the entertainment industry**.

On **July 26, 2019**, he was formally indicted on charges of quasi-rape and quasi-indecent acts by force. On **December 5, 2019**, the Suwon District Court found him **guilty** and sentenced him to **2 years and 6 months imprisonment, suspended for 3 years of probation**, along with 120 hours of social service, 40 hours of sexual offender treatment, and a 3-year employment restriction.

He was replaced by Seo Ji-seok in his final drama, *Joseon Survival Period* (2019), from episode 11 onward.

---

### Summary

Kang Ji-hwan was one of South Korea's prominent actors of the 2000s and early 2010s, celebrated for his versatility across musical theatre, television dramas, and film. His career included acclaimed performances in *Be Strong, Geum-soon!*, *Rough Cut*, *My Girlfriend Is an Agent*, and *Hong Gil-dong*, earning him major acting awards. However, his career came to an abrupt and ignominious end in 2019 following his arrest and conviction for sexual assault, after which he retired from the entertainment industry.

[Source: Wikipedia — "Kang Ji-hwan"]
